title: AL-MIR'AH 99-Name Basis Source

separate_query_dataset.note: This accumulated query dataset is not the 99-entry basis source.

basis_99_v3.json.description: Exact 99-entry, 30-field basis source used by the deployed Space.

poincare_data_v3.json.description: Fitted 99-node Poincare coordinates for the v3 carrier3/layer8/word-span protocol.

abjad_num.description: Stored Abjad total; provisional pending convention adjudication.

level.description: Source hierarchy annotation in the range 0-3; distinct from the fitted three-tier Poincare level.

quranic_verse.description: Verse citation or gloss supplied with the entry.

root_derivatives.description: Illustrative derivatives associated with the root.

wazn_significance.description: Interpretation of the pattern's semantic contribution.

layer2_semantic.description: Extended semantic description used in tool output.

layer3_numerical.description: Numerical interpretation; provisional where it depends on Abjad totals.

paired_opposite.description: Free-text equilibrium-axis annotation; mixes documented and interpretive pairings.

displacement_signature.description: Framework interpretation of isolation or displacement.

ibn_arabi_usage.description: Free-text summary of attributed Ibn Arabi usage.

textual_sources.description: Entry-level source note; not a field-by-field adjudication record.

caveats: Fifteen stored per-Name Abjad totals were flagged by the audit. Stored totals and dependent numerical interpretations are provisional until five orthographic conventions are fixed.
paired_opposite is free text and mixes directly documented axes with interpretive or theoretical opposites; it is not a normalized closed pair registry.
Framework-interpretive fields are released for transparency but are not represented as primary-source facts and are not required to fit query coordinates.
The source JSON level field uses values 0-3. The deployed engine's displayed Dhat/Sifat/Af'al tier is read from poincare_data_v3.json, whose node levels use 0-2.
The source does not contain corpus frequency, seasonal association, color association, or poetic-meter fields formerly listed in the 19-field manuscript draft.
